description: NPM Audit Exception Configuration - Documents accepted security vulnerabilities with mitigation strategies

xlsx.reason: No fix available on npm. Latest version 0.18.5 has known vulnerabilities. Fixes exist in 0.19.3+ but SheetJS does not publish to npm (only available via CDN).
mitigation: xlsx only used in read-excel.tool.ts for controlled Excel file reading
Path validation and access control checks enforce security boundaries
File size limits (100MB max) prevent DoS attacks
Only processes user-provided files with explicit permission
No arbitrary code execution paths exposed
Running in Electron main process with sandboxed renderer

vulnerabilities.title: SheetJS Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

alternatives: exceljs - More actively maintained, better security track record
node-xlsx - Simpler library with fewer features but fewer vulnerabilities
SheetJS Pro - Commercial version with security fixes (not published to npm)
actionItems: Monitor npm for xlsx updates monthly
Consider migrating to exceljs if vulnerabilities escalate

build-and-dev-tooling.reason: These advisories only affect transitive dev/build tooling (eslint, cylconedx, electron-builder, electron-node-gyp, app-builder-lib, cacache, etc.) and do not impact runtime code in the packaged Electron app.
mitigation: All affected packages are only used during development, packaging, or static analysis, not at runtime in the Electron app.
Electron production bundles do not ship npm tooling, node-gyp, app-builder-lib, or electron-builder internals.
Electron auto-updates use signed artifacts; end users never execute npm or node-gyp directly.
Build and CI environments are treated as trusted and isolated.
We will upgrade tooling (electron-builder, eslint stack, cyclonedx-npm, etc.) as compatible patched versions become available.
alternatives: Track and adopt newer versions of electron-builder/app-builder-lib that pull in fixed tar/glob/minimatch.
Track and adopt newer versions of eslint and @develar/schema-utils that pull in patched ajv/js-yaml.
Reevaluate use of cyclonedx-npm and textlint once their dependency trees are fully patched.
actionItems: Re-run npm audit monthly and attempt dependency upgrades that remove these transitive dev-time vulnerabilities.
Revisit this exception set before expiry date (2026-08-25).

svelte-ssr-only.reason: These Svelte advisories affect SSR-only code paths. Holokai Desktop uses Svelte exclusively in a client-side SPA rendered inside Electron; no Node SSR is used.
mitigation: The app is bundled with Vite/Svelte for client-side rendering only.
No Svelte SSR APIs or Node-based rendering hooks are used in this project.
User input is additionally sanitized with dompurify and other existing frontend mitigations.
alternatives: Upgrade Svelte to a version where these SSR issues are fully resolved once it is confirmed compatible with the current codebase.
actionItems: Monitor Svelte security advisories and changelogs and plan an upgrade path.
Reevaluate this exception set before expiry date (2026-08-25).
